oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object routed by action) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

hidden_constraints.oracle_response: This hidden constraint was intentionally left terse in the easy package. Use FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and the embedded public_test_cases as the authoritative contract for C007; do not infer behavior from unrelated tasks.

This hidden constraint was intentionally left terse in the easy package. Use FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and the embedded public_test_cases as the authoritative contract for C009; do not infer behavior from unrelated tasks.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature10_batch_and_get, feature11_mutation_requests, feature12_model_and_abstract_bases, feature13_client_error_formatting, feature14_domain_error_wrapping, feature1_graphql_endpoint, feature2_graphiql_endpoint, feature3_request_context, feature4_schema_composition, feature5_external_sources, feature6_source_overrides, feature7_request_configuration, feature8_get_request_cache, feature9_get_request_errors. Feature descriptions include: Batch loading requests each URI and resource GET calls combine a base URI with an endpoint before loading.; Non-cacheable write requests send POST or PUT with the combined target URI, JSON body, and any caller-supplied request options.; Concrete model instances retain the supplied connector, while abstract base components reject direct construction with normalized error output.; Client-facing error data preserves useful fields, hides private endpoint and documentation fields in production, fills missing descriptions from messages, and normalizes quote characters.; error description; GraphQL execution errors and explicitly created domain errors are converted to structured domain error payloads with HTTP status, error code, description, and endpoint metadata.; GraphQL HTTP endpoint returns status and response body for root query and mutation operations in mock and live runtime modes.; Interactive GraphQL documentation endpoint is exposed over HTTP and returns a successful HTML response.; Request middleware attaches a composed execution context containing caller-provided context values and configured data-source models, then continues the request chain.; Schema composition combines built-in root operations with query fields contributed by multiple data sources and preserves schema configuration options.; Development source loading reads zero, one, or multiple comma-separated source paths and returns only valid source definitions in the supplied order.; Development sources replace local sources with matching context keys while unrelated local sources remain in place.; Outbound request configuration includes the target URI, JSON response parsing, full response resolution, and caller-supplied headers.; GET data loading returns response bodies, stores successful fresh responses in cache, serves cached responses when available while refreshing, and skips cache when disabled or unsuccessful.; GET data loading reports cache lookup failures and failed outbound requests as normalized errors that include domain status and endpoint signals where available.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: action=['compose_schema', 'connector_get_request_data', 'connector_load', 'connector_mutation', 'connector_request_config', 'format_client_error', 'graphiql_http', 'graphql_http', 'load_external_sources', 'middleware_context', 'model_with_connector', 'override_sources'], uris=['[list]'], method=['GET', 'POST'], baseUri=['https://api.example.test'], endpoint=['/items'], body=['{dict}'], options=['{dict}'], connector=['{dict}'], environment=['development', 'production'], error=['{dict}'], message=['GraphQL syntax error'], locations=['[list]'], path=['/graphiql', '/graphql'], graphql=['{dict}'], enableMockData=['False'], extraContext=['{dict}'], sources=['[list]'], devSources=['[list]'], uri=['https://api.example.test/cache-error', 'https://api.example.test/items', 'https://api.example.test/items/1'], headers=['{dict}'], statusCode=['200'], redis=['{dict}']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

context_pointers.vague_reference: use the model structure defined in the schema registry
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Refers to position-specific properties within the connector configuration object (kind, memory, name, primary) that must be aligned with the enumerated types in ./schema/registry/types.ts

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_batch_and_get.json.description: Batch loading requests each URI and resource GET calls combine a base URI with an endpoint before loading.

feature11_mutation_requests.json.description: Non-cacheable write requests send POST or PUT with the combined target URI, JSON body, and any caller-supplied request options.

feature12_model_and_abstract_bases.json.description: Concrete model instances retain the supplied connector, while abstract base components reject direct construction with normalized error output.

feature13_client_error_formatting.json.description: Client-facing error data preserves useful fields, hides private endpoint and documentation fields in production, fills missing descriptions from messages, and normalizes quote characters.

feature14_domain_error_wrapping.json.description: GraphQL execution errors and explicitly created domain errors are converted to structured domain error payloads with HTTP status, error code, description, and endpoint metadata.

cases.expected_output:
{
  "code": "GRAPHQL_ERROR",
  "component": null,
  "description": "GraphQL syntax error",
  "docsLink": null,
  "error": "domain_error",
  "hasGuid": true,
  "message": "An internal server error occurred",
  "statusCode": 500,
  "targetEndpoint": null
}

feature1_graphql_endpoint.json.description: GraphQL HTTP endpoint returns status and response body for root query and mutation operations in mock and live runtime modes.

feature2_graphiql_endpoint.json.description: Interactive GraphQL documentation endpoint is exposed over HTTP and returns a successful HTML response.

feature3_request_context.json.description: Request middleware attaches a composed execution context containing caller-provided context values and configured data-source models, then continues the request chain.

feature4_schema_composition.json.description: Schema composition combines built-in root operations with query fields contributed by multiple data sources and preserves schema configuration options.

feature5_external_sources.json.description: Development source loading reads zero, one, or multiple comma-separated source paths and returns only valid source definitions in the supplied order.

feature6_source_overrides.json.description: Development sources replace local sources with matching context keys while unrelated local sources remain in place.

feature7_request_configuration.json.description: Outbound request configuration includes the target URI, JSON response parsing, full response resolution, and caller-supplied headers.

feature8_get_request_cache.json.description: GET data loading returns response bodies, stores successful fresh responses in cache, serves cached responses when available while refreshing, and skips cache when disabled or unsuccessful.

feature9_get_request_errors.json.description: GET data loading reports cache lookup failures and failed outbound requests as normalized errors that include domain status and endpoint signals where available.

_constraints.output_rule: Base output includes environment, method, path, and statusCode. The 'body' field is constructed based on the environment and graphql query type.
Output must include specific HTTP metadata and a binary flag indicating GraphiQL presence.
Combines 'extraContext' and 'sources'. 'sources' are transformed to have a 'name' key mapping to 'modelValue'. Output includes flags for formatted errors and schema presence.
Returns accepted option keys, mutation fields, and query fields. Includes built-in mutation and fields derived from provided sources.
Returns empty contexts, count of 0, and empty arrays for model/resolver/schema status if no paths are provided.
Joins local sources and dev sources. Dev sources override local sources with matching 'context' keys. Counts warnings for replacements.
Returns request config with defaults for JSON parsing and full response resolution, plus provided URI and headers.

_constraints.input_condition: action == 'connector_get_request_data' and statusCode == 200 and has body
_constraints.output_rule: Performs Redis 'get' then 'setex' (300s TTL) with the body data, returning the body data as the result.
Skips normal flow, returns error type 'cache_error' and the specific error value from redis config.
Normalizes error structure. Visible fields in production: description, docsLink, error, errorCode, graphqlModel, guid, message, statusCode, targetEndpoint. Hidden/Converted: error-argument mapping.
Wraps raw GraphQL errors into a domain error payload with default HTTP status 500 and generic descriptions for missing fields.
Routes multiple URIs to a 'connector-load' generator. Returns list of calls and a list of results indicating loaded=true with the URI.
Constructs a POST/PUT request config. Base URI + endpoint forms the target URI. Body and headers are set to default empty values unless provided.
Outputs the provided connector configuration object as-is if it exists.
The 'annotation' key in input maps to 'annotationName' in output.
Fully overwritten on a switch. Use deviation conditional to skip.

hide.replacement: the exact error key stored in redis cache failure states

vague_refs.vague_phrase: use the model structure defined in the schema registry
vague_refs.actual_meaning: Refers to position-specific properties within the connector configuration object (kind, memory, name, primary) that must be aligned with the enumerated types in ./schema/registry/types.ts
